preamble: The transformation from the voiced alveolar affricate /dz/ to the voiced alveolo-palatal affricate /dʒ/ is a common type of palatalization. This sound change typically occurs when the alveolar affricate is influenced by a following front vowel or palatal sound, causing its place of articulation to shift backward and upward towards the palate.

examples.note: Palatalization of /dz/ to /dʒ/ (written as 'j') before the vowel /i/.

sources: Chafe, W. (2014). A Grammar of the Seneca Language. University of California Press.
